DIAZO
The blueprint your project runs on
How it works Capabilities Demo Request access
Doc. No. DZ-001  ·  Rev. B

One system for the whole project — not just the paperwork.

di·a·zo n. — the master copy a blueprint was printed from. Here: the master plan a project runs on.

Diazo carries a project through every stage gate: documents, procurement, schedule, cost, and closeout. When it's done, it archives the whole thing as a template for the next one. Engineering intelligence runs underneath all of it, reading your specs and drawings so no one has to dig for them.

Try the live demo Talk to us
From kickoff to lessons learned — one system, one source of truth
FIG. 1 — PROJECT LIFECYCLE
SCALE N.T.S.
01 — Process

Your stage gates, not a template's.

Every project's phases are different. Rename, reorder, add, or remove gates below to see how Diazo tracks the sequence you actually run.

Click any phase to rename it — use ‹ › to reorder, × to remove.
02 — Capabilities

Everything a project touches, in one place.

Not a document tool with a project wrapper, and not a task tracker with a PDF viewer bolted on — one system built around how an engineering project actually runs.

CAP. 01
Stage-gate project management
Define phases and gates once, then track a project's progress through each one — with a clear record of what was reviewed and approved before it moved on.
→ Try defining your own gates
CAP. 02
Document control & release
Manage access, versions, and revisions as documents evolve, then compile the final, released set at each gate. Ask questions across the set in plain language, cited to source.
36 doc types
→ See the document registry
CAP. 03
Supply chain & procurement
Build RFP/RFQ packages, send them to vendors, log inquiries and quotes as they come back, and land on a final purchasing plan.
→ Ask about an RFQ
CAP. 04
Tasks, schedule & cost
Recurring tasks generate themselves on schedule. Notifications go out when something's due. Schedule and cost sheets update as work is logged, not at month-end.
→ Ask what's overdue
CAP. 05
Closeout & lessons learned
Run a structured closeout at the end of the project and capture what worked and what didn't, while the team that lived it is still around to say so.
→ See the close-out gate
CAP. 06
Reusable project templates
Everything — phases, document sets, vendor lists, schedules — gets archived as a template, so the next similar project starts from experience, not a blank page.
→ See the archive loop
